title: Mommy Meyer

jokes.text: You don't have the cheekbones for depression.
jokes.setup: Character complaining about wanting to fake their own death
jokes.punchline: Absurd aesthetic critique of their capacity for mental illness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Veep absurdist logic - treating depression as an aesthetic choice requiring proper bone structure. Economical and character-specific.

jokes.text: it's falling apart like a punched wedding cake
jokes.setup: Describing the Families First Bill's poor reception
jokes.punchline: Vivid, absurd metaphor combining violence and celebration
jokes.explanation: Strong visual metaphor that's both specific and absurd. Good economy but could be delivered by any character.

jokes.text: Take this fork, stab me right here in the carotid.
jokes.setup: Complaining about the job being impossible
jokes.punchline: Overly specific suicide request with medical precision
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with anatomical precision fits Selina's character. The specificity of 'carotid' elevates it above generic death wishes.

jokes.text: You are the most influential Mike person in the world.
jokes.setup: Attempting to give Selina a pep talk
jokes.punchline: Malapropism turning 'important' into 'Mike person'
jokes.explanation: Classic Gary malapropism. Character-appropriate but not particularly clever or memorable.

jokes.text: That's basically a quiz on the entire world.
jokes.setup: Foreign policy debate prep
jokes.punchline: Reducing complex geopolitics to elementary school testing
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Selina's reductive view of complex issues. Economical and relatable.

jokes.text: I'm not sure what a fake car would be exactly.
jokes.setup: Defensive about having 'real cars' on his street
jokes.punchline: Literal-minded deconstruction of his own rhetoric
jokes.explanation: Nice deadpan moment where character inadvertently reveals absurdity of his own talking point.

jokes.text: I should be president or something.
jokes.setup: Successfully handling media appearance
jokes.punchline: Casual understatement about highest office in the land
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - the casual 'or something' reveals Tom's ambition while maintaining false modesty.

jokes.text: That would have been a mistake. Huge mistake. It was lacrosse players.
jokes.setup: Suggesting bringing up rumor about O'Brien's daughter and hockey players
jokes.punchline: Correcting the sport while missing the moral point entirely
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection joke. Sets up expectation that Jonah understands it's wrong, then reveals he only cares about accuracy of the sport.

jokes.text: Did you all have tattoos?
jokes.setup: Selina mentions getting 'the old gang back together'
jokes.punchline: Gary's literal interpretation of gang terminology
jokes.explanation: Standard Gary literal-mindedness. In character but predictable.

jokes.text: Did you have to kill a guy? You could pardon yourself now.
jokes.setup: Continuing gang metaphor questions
jokes.punchline: Escalating to murder then casually noting presidential pardon power
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with a smart callback to presidential powers. The casual tone makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Plus we had tits and ass. So we had the whole thing going for us, really.
jokes.setup: Talking about how women 'got it done' in the 90s
jokes.punchline: Reducing female professional success to sexual assets
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about workplace sexism delivered with Selina's characteristic bluntness. Uncomfortable but true to character.

jokes.text: Well, that's fucking not good.
jokes.setup: Learning about shooting with four dead
jokes.punchline: Massive understatement with awkward grammar
jokes.explanation: Classic Selina response - inappropriate understatement mixed with awkward profanity placement.

jokes.text: Yeah, and that they don't demand more gun control.
jokes.setup: Praying for the families of shooting victims
jokes.punchline: Political calculation immediately following sympathy
jokes.explanation: Sharp political satire showing how quickly sympathy turns to damage control. Dark but earned through character.

jokes.text: He's got a thoughts and prayers template.
jokes.setup: Getting Mike to draft statement about shooting
jokes.punchline: Bureaucratic efficiency applied to tragedy response
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satirical concept - the commodification of sympathy into bureaucratic templates. Highly quotable and cutting.

jokes.text: If I keep going at this rate, I'm gonna have gout of the mouth.
jokes.setup: Too many breakfast meetings scheduled
jokes.punchline: Applying rich person's disease to eating schedule
jokes.explanation: Creative metaphor linking excessive eating schedule to aristocratic ailment. Good wordplay and economy.

jokes.text: in a way that is deeply feminist... in a way that's deeply capitalist.
jokes.setup: Explaining how attractive women will lure congressmen
jokes.punchline: Ironic framing of objectification as empowerment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dan - using progressive language to justify regressive practice. The parallel structure enhances the hypocrisy.

jokes.text: Are we at war with clay?
jokes.setup: Questioning why military people are at concrete event
jokes.punchline: Treating building materials as military adversaries
jokes.explanation: Absurdist logic treating construction materials as geopolitical entities. Economical and unexpected.

jokes.text: Oh, I know this. It's candy.
jokes.setup: Asked what the military buys a lot of
jokes.punchline: Confidently wrong answer like game show contestant
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with confident delivery of absurd answer. The enthusiasm makes it funnier.

jokes.text: This place is like a porn shoot with bunting.
jokes.setup: Describing the lobbying atmosphere
jokes.punchline: Comparing professional event to pornography with decorative touch
jokes.explanation: Brilliant juxtaposition of sleazy and wholesome imagery. The specificity of 'bunting' elevates the metaphor.

jokes.text: And it's about as doomed as my donut habit.
jokes.setup: Asking if the Meyer Bill is doomed
jokes.punchline: Comparing legislation to unstoppable food addiction
jokes.explanation: Self-deprecating humor that reveals character weakness while deflecting serious question.

jokes.text: Just like that Jesus guy.
jokes.setup: Bill getting crucified on the Hill
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to crucifixion's most famous victim
jokes.explanation: Casual irreverence toward religious figure fits Mike's character. The understatement 'that Jesus guy' is the key.

jokes.text: put it up on craigslist or something?
jokes.setup: Saying bill doesn't fit
jokes.punchline: Treating legislation like unwanted furniture
jokes.explanation: Domestic metaphor applied to high-stakes politics. Relatable but not particularly sharp.

jokes.text: Well, that's the dream, certainly, but I'm not sure we'll achieve it in this generation.
jokes.setup: O'Brien saying bill will end concept of family
jokes.punchline: Agreeing with dystopian prediction as if it's policy goal
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deadpan delivery treating family destruction as reasonable long-term policy goal. Perfect Selina moment.

jokes.text: Well, that sounds like the world's worst Dr. Seuss book.
jokes.setup: Repeating 'This I had not anticipated'
jokes.punchline: Comparing repetitive crisis response to children's literature
jokes.explanation: Clever cultural reference that captures the absurd repetition. The specificity of 'world's worst' adds impact.

jokes.text: Jesus Christ, Bill, you're shaking like a dog shitting a peach pit.
jokes.setup: Bill nervous during lockdown
jokes.punchline: Hyper-specific simile for trembling
jokes.explanation: Incredibly specific and vivid simile that's both disgusting and hilarious. Highly quotable and original imagery.

jokes.text: Gary, every room you're in is a panic room.
jokes.setup: Gary asking about a panic room
jokes.punchline: Turning Gary's anxiety into architectural feature
jokes.explanation: Perfect character-specific insult that cleverly plays on both Gary's personality and the situation. Excellent economy and structure.

jokes.text: What are you going to do with a clock? Tell him he's late?
jokes.setup: Someone grabbing clock as weapon
jokes.punchline: Absurd non-violent use of timepiece in crisis
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist logic pointing out ineffectiveness of improvised weapon through its mundane function.

jokes.text: He comes here to kill me and he doesn't even know my name?
jokes.setup: Intruder wanted to kill 'that bitch' not using her name
jokes.punchline: Ego injury from anonymous assassination attempt
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina moment - being more offended by lack of recognition than murder attempt. Brilliant character insight.

jokes.text: It's like if dogs could talk.
jokes.setup: Siege stuff going straight to his bladder
jokes.punchline: Comparing his oversharing to hypothetical talking animals
jokes.explanation: Self-aware moment where Mike recognizes his oversharing tendency. The dog comparison is apt but not brilliant.

jokes.text: That's exactly how it was, dumbass.
jokes.setup: Jonah adjusting lighting then being told to change it back
jokes.punchline: His defensive reaction when the lighting returns to original position
jokes.explanation: Jonah's defensive incompetence when caught making unnecessary changes. Character-appropriate but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Sa-shi-mi... Listen to the shus and the mis.
jokes.setup: Correcting pronunciation of sashimi
jokes.punchline: Condescending phonetic breakdown
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Tom's condescending nature. The patronizing tone is key to the humor.

jokes.text: Yeah, help you forget about being molested.
jokes.setup: Someone talking about getting out of the office
jokes.punchline: Richard's inappropriate characterization of the lockdown experience
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that's more uncomfortable than funny. Richard's social inappropriateness on display.

jokes.text: Oh, duck 'cause I ducked. Very clever. Very funny.
jokes.setup: Looking at some visual joke he doesn't understand
jokes.punchline: Fake enthusiasm masking complete confusion
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor from forced laughter at something he clearly doesn't get. The repetition enhances awkwardness.

jokes.text: I'll take all of it.
jokes.setup: Love of tasting menus explained
jokes.punchline: Reducing sophisticated dining to simple gluttony
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment showing Selina's straightforward approach to luxury. Good economy.

jokes.text: Ah, to be 20 years younger, right? I would be in diapers.
jokes.setup: Old man wishing to be younger
jokes.punchline: Taking the math literally to infantile conclusion
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection setup - seems like she's playing along then takes it to absurd literal conclusion.

jokes.text: My God, where were you born, Lake Homo?
jokes.setup: Someone delivering speech effectively
jokes.punchline: Homophobic insult disguised as geographic reference
jokes.explanation: Weak humor relying on outdated homophobic reference. The geographic framing doesn't elevate it much.

jokes.text: Calamity James. And that's just off the top of my head.
jokes.setup: Tom James making PR disaster
jokes.punchline: Instant nickname creation with historical reference
jokes.explanation: Sharp wordplay combining Tom's name with Calamity Jane. The pride in quick wit ('off the top of my head') adds character.

jokes.text: Just get him off, you fucking mannequin.
jokes.setup: Jonah failing to handle the Tom James situation
jokes.punchline: Ben's frustrated insult comparing Jonah to a lifeless display dummy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah insult. 'Mannequin' captures his empty, decorative uselessness perfectly.

jokes.text: Just get him off, you fucking mannequin.
jokes.setup: Jonah not handling crisis well
jokes.punchline: Dehumanizing insult about his ineffectiveness
jokes.explanation: Strong insult that captures both Jonah's appearance and uselessness. 'Mannequin' is perfectly chosen.

jokes.text: Are you trying to hitch a ride? What is that?
jokes.setup: Jonah making ineffective hand gestures off-camera
jokes.punchline: Misinterpreting crisis management as hitchhiking
jokes.explanation: Visual comedy inferred from dialogue. The hitchhiking comparison is apt for ineffective gesturing.

jokes.text: Why the fuck did you bring up balloons if we don't have them?
jokes.setup: Jonah suggesting balloons as example
jokes.punchline: Anger at hypothetical solutions
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how stress makes reasonable brainstorming seem infuriating.

jokes.text: So I'm in the firing line for the second time today.
jokes.setup: Planning opening remarks about assassination attempt
jokes.punchline: Treating serious threat as PR opportunity with pun
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate dark humor turning assassination attempt into press conference fodder. Somewhat predictable.

jokes.text: words don't kill people, unlike guns, which can and did. Which is bad. It's so bad.
jokes.setup: Trying to handle gun control question
jokes.punchline: Fumbling into obvious statements then overselling them
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy from Mike's desperation to say something meaningful but landing on empty platitudes.

jokes.text: Yeah, he just made an enemy of every stupid person with a gun, and there are a lot of those.
jokes.setup: Tom James sympathizing with shooter
jokes.punchline: Casual observation about armed stupidity demographics
jokes.explanation: Dark political commentary delivered with Dan's characteristic bluntness. The matter-of-fact tone enhances impact.

jokes.text: You might be the smartest woman I've ever paid for.
jokes.setup: Complimenting Amy's strategic thinking
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment implying prostitution comparison
jokes.explanation: Very Dan - manages to be both complimentary and insulting simultaneously. Perfect character integration.

jokes.text: I don't have a magic car or whatever he had.
jokes.setup: Saying he's not Merlin
jokes.punchline: Confusing wizard's transportation with King Arthur legend
jokes.explanation: Classic Mike confusion mixing up mythological references. Character-appropriate but not exceptional.

jokes.text: You'll tie a ribbon around Elvis's sagging, rotten ball sack if we ask you to, Mike.
jokes.setup: Mike complaining about impossible PR tasks
jokes.punchline: Grotesquely specific example of degrading job requirements
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly disgusting imagery that captures the degrading nature of Mike's job. Original and memorable.

jokes.text: It's like they took the word brain tumor and changed it to Selina Cyst.
jokes.setup: Complaining about 'Mommy Meyer' nickname
jokes.punchline: Medical metaphor for toxic name association
jokes.explanation: Creative self-deprecating metaphor comparing her name to medical conditions. Good wordplay and self-awareness.

jokes.text: He's going freestyle. God, that is so fucking gangster.
jokes.setup: Tom James abandoning prepared statement
jokes.punchline: Treating political improvisation like hip-hop performance
jokes.explanation: Cultural crossover joke comparing political speech to rap performance. The enthusiasm sells it.

jokes.text: Sir, awesome and amazing had a baby and it grew up to be you.
jokes.setup: Jonah trying to compliment Tom James
jokes.punchline: His overly elaborate and absurd praise
jokes.explanation: Jonah's try-hard compliment that's more awkward than flattering. Shows his desperation to impress.

jokes.text: awesome and amazing had a baby and it grew up to be you.
jokes.setup: Complimenting Tom James's performance
jokes.punchline: Anthropomorphizing adjectives in biological metaphor
jokes.explanation: Typical Jonah over-the-top compliment with weird biological metaphor. In character but clunky execution.

jokes.text: It's like words are your second language, sir.
jokes.setup: Trying to compliment Tom's speaking ability
jokes.punchline: Accidentally suggesting he's bad at English
jokes.explanation: Perfect Richard moment - trying to give compliment but accidentally insulting. Good misdirection structure.

jokes.text: Don't know why I said T. My middle name is John.
jokes.setup: Introducing himself as Richard T. Splett
jokes.punchline: Unnecessarily correcting made-up middle initial
jokes.explanation: Classic Richard overthinking and oversharing. The unnecessary correction is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Surprisingly well. Like vodka and cereal.
jokes.setup: Asked how Tom's apology went down
jokes.punchline: Comparing success to bizarre breakfast combination
jokes.explanation: Unexpected and specific comparison that somehow makes sense. The absurdity of the combination enhances the humor.

jokes.text: Well, construction brings builders. Builders bring sex workers. And sex workers bring STDs. A virtuous circle.
jokes.setup: Explaining pharma guy's presence at concrete event
jokes.punchline: Tracing economic chain from construction to disease
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating chain of capitalism connecting disparate industries. The 'virtuous circle' phrasing is perfect irony.

jokes.text: Plus I had my whole face done.
jokes.setup: Friends complimenting Selina's appearance
jokes.punchline: Her casual admission of extensive plastic surgery
jokes.explanation: Selina's vanity on display. The casual admission followed by immediate backtrack is character-typical.

jokes.text: I have been sober for 27 hours, so let me take that off your hand while I jump off the wagon.
jokes.setup: Friend mentioning 5 years sobriety
jokes.punchline: Comically brief sobriety period ending immediately
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between friend's achievement and Selina's minimal effort. The casual abandonment is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Come on. All I did... I just went... How can anybody not love him? He's like your dad except he's nice.
jokes.setup: Praising Tom James's likability
jokes.punchline: Casual insult to listener's father as comparison
jokes.explanation: Jonah's social obliviousness leads to casual insult disguised as compliment. Character-appropriate.

jokes.text: I like my bourbon like I like my women... 18 years old and wet.
jokes.setup: Standard 'like my women' joke format
jokes.punchline: Creepy sexual preference disguised as alcohol preference
jokes.explanation: Tired joke format with creepy punchline. Fits Kent's awkwardness but relies on shock value over craft.

jokes.text: I'm not. I mean, he wasn't gay. He was just an asshole.
jokes.setup: Selina apologizing for the death
jokes.punchline: Anna's matter-of-fact correction about her dead husband
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from the death reveal. Her casual dismissal of her dead husband is appropriately dark.

jokes.text: What's for dessert, a tub of ice cream and four spoons?
jokes.setup: Pizza dinner like old broke days
jokes.punchline: Extending poverty nostalgia to dessert
jokes.explanation: Good callback to shared poverty experience. The specific imagery of communal ice cream eating is relatable.

jokes.text: Fuck my face.
jokes.setup: Realizing she just publicly advocated drug legalization
jokes.punchline: Selina's profane expression of panic
jokes.explanation: Selina's panic expressed through her typical crude language. Effective but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Fuck my face.
jokes.setup: Realizing she admitted wanting to legalize drugs
jokes.punchline: Vulgar expression of self-directed anger
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina reaction - vulgar, immediate, and economical. The self-directed nature makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Thank God, because tax law was never your strength.
jokes.setup: Selina saying she has the finest economic minds working on her bill
jokes.punchline: Friend's casual reminder of Selina's academic weaknesses
jokes.explanation: Good undermining of Selina's authority by someone who knew her before she was powerful.

jokes.text: What's 15%? What's 15%?
jokes.setup: Discussing Selina's tax law weaknesses
jokes.punchline: Mocking past mathematical incompetence
jokes.explanation: Character history callback that reinforces long-standing weaknesses. The repetition suggests it was memorable.

jokes.text: The Meyer campaign used the health records of dead children to target and appeal to voters who were recently bereaved parents.
jokes.setup: Finally revealing the data breach details
jokes.punchline: Grotesquely specific exploitation of child death
jokes.explanation: Shocking revelation that's both horrifying and perfectly plausible for this universe. The clinical description makes it funnier.

jokes.text: The Meyer campaign used the health records of dead children to target and appeal to voters who were recently bereaved parents.
jokes.setup: Mike being pressured to reveal the data breach
jokes.punchline: The horrific details of exploiting dead children for political gain
jokes.explanation: Shock humor from the extreme awfulness of the revelation. The specificity makes it more horrifying and darkly funny.

jokes.text: You know, those kids have been dead for a while.
jokes.setup: Trying to minimize data breach severity
jokes.punchline: Absurd logic that time makes child exploitation acceptable
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly awful logic delivered with bureaucratic matter-of-factness. The casual tone makes the horror funnier.

jokes.text: You know, those kids have been dead for a while.
jokes.setup: Mike revealing the dead children data breach
jokes.punchline: Kent's attempt to minimize the scandal through temporal distance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kent line - emotionally tone-deaf rationalization delivered with complete sincerity. Among the episode's best jokes.

jokes.text: I'd like to press his meat.
jokes.setup: Reference to Tom James on 'Meet the Press'
jokes.punchline: Sexual double entendre from show title
jokes.explanation: Basic sexual wordplay on show title. Economical but not particularly clever or original.

jokes.text: It's really difficult talking to you now that you're sober.
jokes.setup: Deborah being critical while sober
jokes.punchline: Selina's honest assessment of sober friendship
jokes.explanation: Brutally honest observation about how sobriety changes relationships. Dark but insightful.

jokes.text: No, Gary, it's the same one. He's doing a victory lap.
jokes.setup: Gary asking about another intruder during the second lockdown
jokes.punchline: Selina's casual description of the assassination attempt as a celebration
jokes.explanation: 'Victory lap' for an assassination attempt is brilliantly dark. Treats mortal threat as sports achievement.

jokes.text: I shook Jack Nicholson's hand and I thought, 'If this hand could talk, it would say 'wash me.''
jokes.setup: Meeting famous actor
jokes.punchline: Focusing on hand hygiene over celebrity encounter
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gary moment - missing the significance of celebrity meeting for mundane concern. Good character specificity.

jokes.text: Well, if the intruder kills you, Mike, then who's gonna announce your death?
jokes.setup: Mike wishing the intruder would kill him
jokes.punchline: Amy's practical concern about press management logistics
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor about government logistics. The practical concern about who announces deaths is perfectly cynical.

jokes.text: Your face looks kind of like a sad egg.
jokes.setup: Trying to comfort Mike
jokes.punchline: Unhelpful visual comparison to breakfast food
jokes.explanation: Perfectly Richard - trying to help but delivering bizarre and unhelpful observation. The specificity makes it memorable.

jokes.text: The only person who's interested in killing you, Deborah, is here in this room.
jokes.setup: Deborah panicking about the intruder
jokes.punchline: Selina's 'reassurance' that only she wants to kill Deborah
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina - turning reassurance into threat. The casual admission of murderous intent is darkly funny.

jokes.text: The only person who's interested in killing you, Deborah, is here in this room.
jokes.setup: Friend panicking about intruder
jokes.punchline: Casual death threat disguised as reassurance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina - turning comfort into veiled threat. The matter-of-fact delivery enhances the dark humor.

tmdb_overview: The president is shaken by a dramatic incident at the White House.
insight: Family dynamics episode trades Veep's usual pace for character depth, landing just below excellence.
